seo.title: Website Cost and Web Design FAQ in Nova Scotia | Hatt Digital
seo.description: Straight answers for Nova Scotia businesses about website pricing, timelines, ownership, payment, and local SEO. Most sites cost $500 to $1,500.

hero.heading: Straight answers, no fluff.
hero.lead: The questions I get asked most, answered plainly. If yours is not here, just ask.

items.q: How much does a website cost?
items.a: Most Starter sites land between $500 and $800, and most Business sites land between $900 and $1,500. Bigger builds with bookings or extra pages are quoted by scope. Either way, you get one flat, all in number before work starts. The price we agree on is the price you pay.

items.q: How long does a website take?
items.a: Most sites go live within two to three weeks once I have your content, photos, text, and logo. A simple one page site can be ready in about a week. Bigger projects with bookings or extra pages take a little longer, and you will have a timeline up front.

items.q: Can you redesign my current website?
items.a: Yes. Redesigns are a big part of what I do. If your current site is slow, dated, hard to use on a phone, or not helping people contact you, I can rebuild it into something cleaner, faster, and easier to manage.

items.q: Do you help with Google and SEO?
items.a: Yes. I build the local SEO basics into every site, including clean page titles, location wording, proper structure, fast loading pages, and mobile friendly layouts. That helps nearby customers find and understand your business when they search. I cannot promise guaranteed rankings, but I can set the site up properly.

items.q: Can I update the website myself later?
items.a: Yes, self editing is available with the Business plan and Custom plans. I can set the site up so you can make simple edits to text and images yourself, and I include a plain English guide. If you choose the Starter plan, I keep things simpler and can handle small updates for you if needed.

items.q: Do I need to have all my text and images ready?
items.a: No. An idea of what your business does and what you want the site to help with is enough to start. I can guide you on photos, wording, and what information is worth including.

items.q: Why not just use Squarespace or Wix?
items.a: Squarespace and Wix can be useful, especially for simple websites. The problem is that most business owners do not have time to turn a template into a site that looks professional, loads well, explains the service clearly, and helps people get in touch. These platforms can also become limiting when you want more control over design, speed, SEO structure, custom features, or how the site grows later. You can build it yourself, but if you want it planned, written, designed, and set up properly, that is what I handle.

items.q: Do I actually own my website?
items.a: Yes. Your domain, website, and content are in your name. You are not locked in, and there are no fees to access your own property. It is yours.

items.q: I am not techy. Is that a problem?
items.a: Not at all. I handle the technical side and explain everything in plain English. You focus on running your business, and I take care of the website.

items.q: What happens after I contact you?
items.a: I reply within one business day with honest advice and clear next steps. We will have a quick, no pressure chat about what you need, then I will send a flat quote and timeline. Nothing starts until you are happy with the plan.

mini.heading: Still have a question?
mini.body: Send me a message and I’ll give you a straight answer before you commit to anything.

cta.heading: Still weighing it up? Tell me about your website.
cta.body: Share your business, your goals, and a budget you’re comfortable with, and I’ll come back with a clear, fair quote — no pressure either way.
